strengths: best foot archers in the game — extra range on Longbowmen and Arbalests
cheap Town Centers from Castle Age accelerate booming
strong Paladins and Halberdiers for army diversity
excellent late-game eco via cheap TCs
weaknesses: no Bloodlines — cavalry have less HP than average
no Hussar upgrade — vulnerable to late-game raiding
no Siege Engineers — shorter-ranged Trebuchets and siege
predictable gameplan — opponents expect and prepare for mass archers
unique_unit: Longbowman — foot archer with exceptional range; fully upgraded reaches 11 range, outranging virtually everything including towers

unique_tech.castle_age: Yeomen — +1 range for foot archers and +2 attack for towers
unique_tech.imperial_age: Warwolf — Trebuchets deal blast damage, killing units clustered near targeted buildings

economy_bonuses: Shepherds work 25% faster — strong early food income on sheep-heavy maps
Town Centers cost -50% wood from Castle Age onward — enables aggressive booming
Market fees reduced — easier to convert resources when needed
build_order_notes: Fast Castle into cheap TC boom while transitioning to mass Longbowmen or Arbalests. Longbowmen outrange towers and enemy archers — use that to siege safely. Pair with Knights or Halberdiers to cover melee weaknesses.
common_pairings: Mayans — double archer output; Mayans cover raiding weakness with Eagle Warriors
Lithuanians — top-tier Paladins cover the cavalry gap while Britons provide ranged support
Khmer — Khmer siege and elephants combined with Briton archers is overwhelming

power_spikes.feudal_age: Moderate — scout and archer opening is solid but not exceptional
power_spikes.castle_age: Strong — Longbowmen online, cheap TCs allow fast boom
power_spikes.imperial_age: Very strong — Arbalest plus Warwolf Trebs plus Paladins is a complete army
